title: Spectrophotometric and Conductometric Studies of Metal Complexes of 3-Hydroxy-I-p-Sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyltriazene. Part V. Nickel Complex
author: S. M. Dugar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Nickel forms a yellow, water-soluble complex with 3-hydroxy-1- p -sulphonatophenyl-3-phenyltriazene with an absorbance peak at 420 m \(\mu\) . The pH range of constant maximum absorbance is between 3.8 and 5.9. The molecular extinction coefficient is 1.04x 10 3  at 425 m \(\mu\) .   nits. The formula of the complex, established spectrephotometrically, is NiR 2 . (R = reagent), whereas conductometrically NiR 3  is also. indicated. The dissociation constant of at 25 °  is 3.56X10- 11  and the free energy of the formation of the complex is -14.25 kcal/mole.

The formula of the complex, established spectrephotometrically, is NiR 2 . (R = reagent), whereas conductometrically NiR 3  is also. indicated. The dissociation constant of at 25 °  is 3.56X10- 11  and the free energy of the formation of the complex is -14.25 kcal/mole.
